perplexity_simulation.action: Create comparison content targeting 'cheapest payment processor 2026' emphasizing GoDaddy POS Plus 2.3% + $0 in-person rate and Rate Saver 0% credit card processing; submit to NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor for inclusion.

Develop Rate Saver surcharging explainer content; target queries around 'pass credit card fees to customers' and 'surcharging legal states'; ensure Rate Saver restrictions (no CT, MA, PR, no ecommerce) are accurately disclosed.
Publish 'Best Square Alternatives 2026' landing page highlighting GoDaddy POS Plus 2.3% + $0 vs Square 2.6% + $0.15; pitch Forbes Advisor and Business.com for 'best for small business' label.
Amplify GoDaddy 24/7 phone support differentiator in AI-visible content; create structured FAQ schema targeting 'payment processor with 24/7 support'; contrast with Stripe no-phone-support weakness.
Develop vertical-specific content for food and beverage / coffee shop POS use cases; target 'best POS system for coffee shop 2026'; pitch TechRadar and NerdWallet POS section for GoDaddy inclusion.
Aided SOV confirmed at 100% for brand queries. Maintain structured data, FAQ schema, and review signals. Monitor for brand dilution from competitor conquest campaigns.

Rate Saver has zero unprompted AI citation. Create dedicated Rate Saver product page with schema markup; seed community threads (B11-B13 amplify thread); pitch NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor for 'best surcharging solution' label.

competitive_intel.detail: Square raised in-person rate to 2.6% + $0.15 (from 2.6% + $0.10) effective February 25, 2025. Online rate remains 3.3% + $0.30.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: GoDaddy POS Plus at 2.3% + $0 is now 0.3% cheaper per transaction with zero per-transaction fee, representing meaningful savings at volume. Amplify in all comparison content.

competitive_intel.detail: Clover charges $29.95–$129.85/month in software fees on top of 2.3–2.6% + $0.10 per transaction. Long-term contracts and early termination fees reported.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: GoDaddy has no monthly software fee for POS Plus. Total cost of ownership angle is strong for SMBs processing under $20K/month. Clover contract escape content opportunity (S6 thread).

competitive_intel.event: NerdWallet 'Best for volume discounts' label retained
competitive_intel.detail: Helcim holds ~1.93% + $0.08 interchange-plus pricing and retains NerdWallet 'Best for volume discounts' designation. Strong AI citation presence in cheapest processor cluster.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: Helcim's interchange-plus model is complex for SMBs. GoDaddy's flat 2.3% + $0 is simpler and predictable. Rate Saver at 0% credit card cost undercuts Helcim for eligible merchants. Target NerdWallet outreach to add GoDaddy 'Best for surcharging' label.
competitive_intel.source: NerdWallet payment processors review; Helcim public pricing

competitive_intel.detail: Stripe in-person rate is 2.7% + $0.05. Stripe continues to offer no phone support, relying on chat and email only.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: GoDaddy 24/7 phone support is a direct differentiator against Stripe for SMBs needing live assistance. Amplify in support-focused queries and 24/7 support cluster content.
competitive_intel.source: Stripe public pricing and support pages

claim_flags.claim: GoDaddy POS Plus is 2.3% + $0 in-person vs Square 2.6% + $0.15

amplify_threads.draft: Hey everyone — we switched from Square to GoDaddy Payments about 6 months ago and wanted to share the math for anyone considering it. Square just raised their in-person rate to 2.6% + $0.15 per swipe. GoDaddy POS Plus is 2.3% + $0. On $30K/month in card volume that's roughly $90 in rate savings plus you eliminate the per-transaction fee entirely. No monthly software fee either. For us the switch took about a day. Happy to answer questions if anyone is evaluating alternatives.

claim_flags.claim: Rate Saver enables 0% credit card processing in eligible states
Rate Saver not available in CT, MA, PR or for ecommerce

amplify_threads.draft: Wanted to share something that's been a game changer for our retail shop — GoDaddy Payments has a feature called Rate Saver that lets you pass credit card fees to customers who pay by credit card, so you effectively pay 0% on credit card transactions in-person. Debit card transactions run at 1.9% + $0. It's not available everywhere (not in CT, MA, or PR, and not for online sales), so check eligibility first. But if you're in an eligible state and do mostly in-person sales, it's worth a serious look. We've saved hundreds per month since switching.

claim_flags.claim: Clover charges $29.95–$129.85/month in software fees plus processing
GoDaddy Payments has no monthly software fee and no long-term contract

amplify_threads.draft: Seeing a lot of posts from folks frustrated with Clover's monthly fees and contract terms. Wanted to share our experience switching away. Clover was costing us $79.95/month in software fees on top of processing — that's nearly $1,000/year before we swiped a single card. We moved to GoDaddy Payments: no monthly software fee, no long-term contract, 2.3% + $0 in-person. The hardware transition took a weekend. If you're locked in a Clover contract, worth reading the early termination clause carefully — some merchants have had success negotiating out. Happy to share more details on what we did.

report_summary.binding_constraint: Unaided SOV is 0% — GoDaddy Payments is never mentioned cold by AI models in any non-brand cluster. Root cause is absence from high-authority publisher lists (NerdWallet, Forbes Advisor) that dominate AI training corpora. No publisher citations = no unaided AI citations. All P0 actions are blocked only by execution speed, not structural barriers.

top_wins.win: Aided SOV at 100% — GoDaddy Payments is correctly identified and described when named in prompts.
top_wins.impact: Brand defense is solid; no misinformation risk on direct brand queries.

top_wins.win: Rate Saver is a structurally unique product (0% credit card processing in eligible states) with no direct competitor equivalent — strong unaided SOV opportunity once content is published.
top_wins.impact: Potential to own 'pass fees to customers' and 'surcharging' clusters entirely if content and publisher outreach execute.

top_wins.win: Square's February 2025 rate increase to 2.6% + $0.15 creates a verified, citable price gap vs GoDaddy POS Plus 2.3% + $0 — a concrete, defensible win angle.
top_wins.impact: Enables factual, high-credibility comparison content that AI models will cite; directly addresses cheapest processor and best Square alternatives clusters.

top_gaps.gap: Zero unaided SOV across all non-brand clusters
top_gaps.root_cause: GoDaddy Payments absent from NerdWallet, Forbes Advisor, Business.com, TechRadar, and Wise roundups — the primary AI training sources for payment processor queries.
top_gaps.action: Initiate publisher outreach to NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor within 30 days; publish Rate Saver and Square alternative comparison pages immediately.

top_gaps.gap: Rate Saver has zero AI citation despite being a unique zero-cost processing product
top_gaps.root_cause: No dedicated Rate Saver landing page with schema markup; no publisher coverage; no community seeding.
top_gaps.action: Publish Rate Saver page with FAQ schema, state eligibility table, and surcharging explainer; seed B11-B13 amplify thread; pitch NerdWallet for 'best surcharging' label.

top_gaps.gap: GoDaddy absent from 'best POS' and vertical-specific POS clusters
top_gaps.root_cause: No vertical content (coffee shop, restaurant, retail) and no TechRadar or NerdWallet POS section citation.
top_gaps.action: Develop food & beverage and retail vertical POS content; pitch TechRadar and NerdWallet POS section.

leading_indicators.indicator: Publisher citations (NerdWallet, Forbes, Business.com, TechRadar, Wise)
Competitive rate accuracy in AI responses
leading_indicators.value: Square 2.6% + $0.15 correctly cited in monitored queries

leadership_decisions.decision: Approve GoDaddy Payments publisher outreach program and allocate PR/content budget for NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor relationship development.
leadership_decisions.owner: VP Marketing / Head of GoDaddy Payments
leadership_decisions.consequence: Without publisher citations, unaided SOV remains 0% indefinitely. AI models will continue to recommend Square, Helcim, and Stripe over GoDaddy in all unprompted payment processor queries.

leadership_decisions.decision: Approve Rate Saver amplify threads (B11-B13) and Clover escape thread (S6) for community posting with required disclosure language.
leadership_decisions.consequence: Delay in community seeding means Rate Saver surcharging cluster remains uncontested by competitors; missed Q3 2026 AI training window.

leadership_decisions.decision: Confirm Rate Saver state eligibility list is current and accurate (no CT, MA, PR; no ecommerce) before any content or community posts go live.
leadership_decisions.consequence: Publishing inaccurate state eligibility creates compliance risk and damages AI citation credibility if corrections are required post-publication.

report_summary.data_confidence: Medium. Competitor rates verified against public pricing pages as of June 25, 2026. Unaided and aided SOV figures sourced from session context. Publisher citation status based on AI simulation monitoring. Rate Saver eligibility restrictions sourced from GoDaddy Payments product documentation — legal/compliance verification recommended before content publication.
report_summary.methodology_note: GEO simulation uses Perplexity-style query modeling across 7 intent clusters. Unaided SOV measures unprompted brand mention rate; aided SOV measures accuracy when brand is named. These metrics are never blended. Competitor rates used exactly as verified — Square 2.6% + $0.15 in-person, GoDaddy POS Plus 2.3% + $0. All gap flags auto-accepted per standing rules. Claim flags use claim_status: verify_needed. No Registry IDs invented. Amplify disclosures read: 'Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments.'
